observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to perform verification of the reconciling items and prepare the adjusting journal entries to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust as appropriate, the erroneous recordings made and henceforth, record the advance, progress and final payments for infrastructure projects using the appropriate asset accounts, in accordance with Section 81, Chapter 3, Volume III and Section 50, Chapter 3, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs, and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:     
                                                                

a. Ensure that funds received are supported with duly signed MOA or related documents;                                                                                                                                                          

b. Coordinate and seek advice with the source agencies for the previously recorded Due to LGUS and Due to NGAS which remained outstanding for more than 10 years, to ascertain the propriety and purpose of the funds and implement and/or refund the balances as appropriate, otherwise, consider requesting for its write-off pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and                      

c. Reconcile the erroneous entries affecting the Due to LGUs and Due to NGAS accounts, and effect the necessary adjustments to ensure the correct and reliable presentation of the year-end balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Index Card from the Assessor's Office and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth recognize in the books the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes as submitted by the Municipal Treasurer's Office.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the amount recognized as Receivables and Deferred Income at year-end to exclude fines and penalties.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the MDRRMO and concerned officials to ensure optimum utilization of the LDRRM Fund and full implementation of its programs, projects and activities to strengthen the Municipality's disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We likewise recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the MDRRMO to refrain from charging against the LDRRMF, expenditure items that are not DRRM related, and instead provide budgetary support to the MDRRM Office for these expenditure items which should be covered by the general fund of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed:

a.     That the MENRO Designate and concerned offices provide assistance to the barangays in the management of their solid waste and actively promote and enforce the sorting and segregation of wastes at the household level and all other sources to ensure that only non-recyclable materials and special wastes are collected by the Municipality for proper and efficient storage in its facilities, pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003;


b.    That the MENRO Designate establish stringent rules or SOPs outlining the protocols for tasks within the ESWM Center and strictly enforce the mandatory use of PPEs by all workers to ensure their safety and well- being while carrying out their duties; and

c.    To establish a policy for the generation of revenue from saleable recyclables, ensure that collections are properly remitted to the Municipality, and expenditures undergo the proper authorization and accounting of disbursements from public funds.


We further recommended that the MENRO Designate and the members of the Solid Waste Management Board review and update its 10-year SWM Plan and prepare a comprehensive work and financial plan relative to solid waste management operations to establish the timing and extent of implementation of each program, project and activities during the year, and to employ monitoring mechanisms to ensure full implementation thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GAD Focal Person and concerned offices henceforth ensure the submission to the Auditor of the supporting schedules with complete details on the list of expenditures reported in the GAD AR to ascertain the status of implementation and propriety of expenditures determined as GAD-related.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GFPS of the Municipality to set the GAD Agenda or identify priority gender issues to be addressed by the LGU during the three-year term of its leadership and use this as a basis for the annual formulation of PPAs to be included in the GPB so that PPAs in the GPB be gender responsive and supportive of the gender issues of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the concerned offices to ensure that all funds received from source agencies NGAs and LGUs are supported with duly signed Memorandum of Agreement and/or related documents and a copy is furnished to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate and seek advice with the source agencies for the previously recorded Due to LGUs and NGAs which remained outstanding for more than 10 years, in order to ascertain the proper treatment/usage of the funds and utilize the same as appropriate.

We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge - Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to: 


Intensify its collection efforts for the uncollected stall fees and to henceforth ensure that all rentals are supported by a valid and binding lease contract; 


Reconcile the variance noted in the monthly rates per master list of stall occupants and the Revised Revenue Code, and indicate the same in the Contract of Lease; and 




Ensure that the granting of Contract of Lease as well as, the levy and collection of taxes and fees is in accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code. 

Xxx

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan revisit the Municipal Ordinance to check whether rates and terms and conditions are still applicable and fair enough and update when found necessary to expand the implementation of revenue collection.

We recommended and it was agreed that the MDRRM Council review and update the LDRRMFIP annually and include under a separate caption, the list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years and incorporate the same in the AIP of the Municipality;

We maintained our recommendation that the Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories, prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books and to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to ensure the liquidation of all unliquidated cash advances, and henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with unsettled accounts pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445.

We also recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to verify, analyze and validate the accounts and identify those eligible for write-off and comply with the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Lastly, we recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary JEV to properly classify the cash advances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the:

Recipients concerned to refund the over payment or unauthorized amount received; and henceforth, strictly observe the limitations provided in DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-01 dated March 23, 2020; and

Personnel concerned to submit a copy of the document supporting the authorized working arrangement and the payroll with complete signatures of recipients and required signatories.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other personnel concerned submit the required documentary requirements to support the disbursements immediately to the Office of the Auditor to avoid suspension of the same, pursuant to Sections 4(6) and 39 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We also recommended that the LGU submit thru the personnel concerned the following documents: 

Office Order/Special Order/Authority to Report to Office/Workstation during Proclamation declaring the State of Public Health Emergency of all Municipal Officials /employees including the job hire personnel granted with SRA; and 


Sanggunian Bayan Resolution determining the amount to be granted to each employee taking into consideration LGUs financial capability.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce full settlement of the unsettled amount of the year 2015 disallowances by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due to the person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance pursuant to the prescribed Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as provided in Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 or request for authority from the Commission for the settlement of the disallowances by installment pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2017-021 dated November 3, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents/ papers pursuant   to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995. Henceforth, the official involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts shall turn over the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from dated receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
a. 	Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the RPCPPE under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b.	GSO Designate to update and maintain Property Cards and for the Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate to work together in reconciling and updating their records. Thereafter, make necessary adjustments to any discrepancies noted.
Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the processing and completion of land titling in the name of the Municipality to establish legal ownership.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to conduct a thorough reconciliation of land records and thereafter effect adjustments in the Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Disposal and Appraisal Committee to expedite the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration and the resulting loss in value.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the carrying amount of unserviceable properties after the disposal and recognize the gain or loss arising therefrom.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and account for the discrepancy totaling (₱0.754) million between the Cashbook and Subsidiary Ledger on Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Current Account, and Cash – Local Treasury.
We likewise recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to validate, adjust accounting records and effect the adjustments in the Cash in Bank account of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review and verification of the above-mentioned reconciling items to effect the adjustments appearing in the BRS to reflect the adjusted and correct balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account; and
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the OIC- Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and other officials concerned to validate and adjust accounting records accordingly based on the current and correct status of the three cash in bank accounts in GF PNB xxxxxxxxxx12, TF LBP xxxx-xxxx51, and TF PVB xxxx-xxxxxx-9.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to record the Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) receivables at the beginning of each year, based on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes prepared by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office using data from the Assessor’s Office.
We also recommended and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare this certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible for the year—based on records from the Assessor’s Office—and submit it to the Municipal Accountant at the start of the year to ensure timely and accurate recording of receivables.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Conduct a thorough review and verification of all outstanding balances under the "Due to NGAs" account to determine the validity and source of these amounts, particularly the ₱360,830.92 balance from prior years where supporting documents are lacking;
Assess the necessity of those unexpended fund balances. If it is determined that the said funds are no longer needed, they must be returned to the source agencies in accordance with applicable laws and regulations. If deemed necessary, management should seek the appropriate authorization from the source agencies for possible realignment or utilization of the funds for other related municipal projects; and
Implement a system to monitor the status of funds, ensure the prompt implementation of the PPAs, and submit the liquidation reports and refund the unutilized funds on time.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:

a) Municipal Accountant to:
Follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of inventories in compliance with Section 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs Volume I; and
Record stock issuances only upon receipt of the SSMI with the original copy of the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS).

b) General Services Office-Designate to:
Maintain stock cards for supplies to track their receipt and disposal and reconcile them with the accounting unit's ledger cards; and
Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the RIS, to the Municipal Accountant for recording of the cost of the supplies and materials issued.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify and record the penalties under the "Tax Revenue - Fines and Penalties - Property Taxes" account.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through its LDRRMO and LDRRM council, to develop and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP that covers the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management and identify programs, projects, and activities that focus on disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation and recovery.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from charging non-priority items from the 5% LDRRMF, and formulate/implement DRRM programs, projects and activities that are congruent with the thematic areas of disaster reduction ad management to strengthen and enhance the Municipality’s disaster capabilities at all levels.
Finally, we recommended and the Management agreed to establish a separate budget for the MDRRMO under the General Fund to support its operational requirements, thereby minimizing the use of the LDRRMF for administrative and non-DRRM purposes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRM and the affected equity account in the General Fund books, pursuant to item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, so that year-end valuation of financial statement balances will be presented accurately. Henceforth, strictly observe the guidelines on the treatment of unexpended LDRRMF balances, particularly for Capital Outlay, ensuring that such balances remain in the General Fund until the completion of the related projects.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the concerned officials agreed to establish the LDRRMO in accordance with the mandatory positions specified in NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 to ensure the effective implementation of programs and activities related to disaster risk reduction management.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that all PPAs funded through the 20% Development Fund are clearly and specifically identified in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP), in line with existing budget preparation and authorization guidelines, to prevent broad discretionary spending and to strengthen project monitoring and accountability.
We likewise recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to secure the required prior authorization from the Local Sanggunian before entering into contracts funded under the 20% Development Fund.
Finally, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper accounting treatment of mobilization fees by recording them under the appropriate "Advances to Contractors" account, net of applicable taxes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to strengthen internal control to see to it that the net take-home pay of the employees shall not be less than ₱5,000.00 and in cases where the total authorized deductions shall reduce the net take-home pay to less than five thousand pesos (₱5,000.00), deductions shall be made in accordance with the order of preference mentioned in the GAA.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to establish the Municipality’s GAD M&E Team and formalize the monitoring and evaluation of its GAD-related activities through the issuance of an appropriate Executive Order. The GAD M&E system should be designed to track the progress of GAD PPAs, document challenges and corresponding solutions, and provide regular reports on the Municipality’s GAD implementation efforts.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct: 

a. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to provide to the Municipal Accountant a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at year end; and      

b. The Municipal Accountant to issue a JEV reverting the unreleased checks at year-end to the Cash in Bank account and record the appropriate payable account based on the schedule provided by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize the assets using the appropriate asset account as well as, the corresponding depreciation, and verify that funds were fully liquidated to source agencies to ensure the propriety and accuracy of the debits made to the Due to LGUS and Due to NGAS accounts, so that the correct balance of the total assets, total liabilities and government equity are properly reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the Assets in the books using the appropriate asset account as well as, the corresponding depreciation, and henceforth ensure the proper recording of the infrastructure assets whether in the process of construction or already completed and the related depreciation, in accordance with Section 50, Chapter 3, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUS, COA Circular No. 2015-009, and IPSAS 17.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Status of Work Accomplished as of September 24, 2023 and compute the unperformed portion of works as of target completion date, and the Municipal Accountant to compute for the liquidated damages to be imposed to the delayed contractor.

We likewise recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to henceforth ensure the timely inspection of completed projects to ascertain the actual accomplishment and make certain that the actual date of completion is stated in the Certificate of Project Completion for the Municipal Accountant to properly determine the timeliness of project implementation and to deduct in the final payments and/or impose to the erring contractors, payment for liquidated damages if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

Direct the Municipal Engineer and the concerned offices to ensure that all projects are procurement-and-implementation ready to make certain its timely implementation and completion and the 20% Development Fund is maximized in accordance with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1; and

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to see to it that the date of effectivity of contracts is clearly stated in the Notice to Proceed and ensure consistency of information provided in the supporting documents, to properly ascertain the timeliness of project implementation.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor to check the status of real properties covered by CARP, request an updated list of the properties subject to RPT from the Department of Agrarian Reform, and reconcile the reports to accurately reflect the taxable real properties.

We also recommended and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer agreed to monitor and maintain a comprehensive report on the collection of real property taxes due for the current year, and enforce remedies provided under Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent RPT/SET.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the creation of a Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal process of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 379 of RA No. 7160, Section 125, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 as updated by COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth see to it that requests for advance payment by contractors are properly supported with the financial instruments required under Section 4, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before payments are made.

We likewise recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth comply with the conditions stated under Section 6, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the withholding of retention money and the substitution thereof with the allowed financial instruments.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth make certain that warranty security for goods procured is withheld or submitted in the required form before payment is made, pursuant to Section 62 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We maintained our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the proper recording of the transfer to the Trust Fund books of the unexpended balance of CY 2022 QRF and DRRM-MOOE and henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the accounting of unutilized balances of the LDRRMF.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the nature of the erroneous accounts and make the necessary adjustments to correct the same, and henceforth strictly observe the proper recording of funds received to ensure reliability of reported balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Agriculturist and concerned offices to submit the lacking documents mentioned, coordinate with the DA as to the proper implementation of the project, and henceforth ensure that there is proper utilization of funds in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement and pertinent documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and the end-user units to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through implementation 
of the identified priority development projects in compliance with Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, and to oversee and closely monitor the timely implementation of these projects. 

We also recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Development Council and end user units to henceforth ensure that all projects to be implemented during the year should be procurement-and-implementation ready to make certain its timely implementation and completion.

We recommended and the MDRRM Council and other concerned officials agreed to reassess all PPAs included in the LDRRMF current and continuing appropriations which remained unimplemented to date and determine the necessity to implement the same otherwise, reprogram to priority PPAs and ensure its full 
implementation. It was also agreed that the concerned offices make certain the propriety of all charges against the fund, in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 in order to ensure the proper utilization of funds and to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE require the concerned personnel to submit immediately the Monthly Report of Official Travels with the corresponding Monthly Report of Consumption of Fuel, Oil and Lubricants for each vehicle for CY 2020 to the Audit Team for verification and determination of the reasonableness of the charges made to Fuel, Oil & Lubricants Expenses and henceforth, to submit the said reports within a reasonable period every month thereafter.
We recommended that all government-owned motor vehicles be kept in the garage provided by the office.

We recommended and Management has agreed that:

The Inventory Committee establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its properties by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE in compliance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386;

The Municipal Accountant and GSO In-Charge maintain and regularly update their respective reports namely, PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; and



The Municipal Accountant and GSO In-Charge perform a reconciliation of the reported PPEs per physical count, accounting and property records in order to establish the correct balance of the PPE accounts.

Xxx

The Municipality consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We maintained our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the proper recording of the transfer to the Trust Fund books of the unexpended balance of CY 2022 QRF and DRRM-MOOE and henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the accounting of unutilized balances of the LDRRMF.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the nature of the erroneous accounts and make the necessary adjustments to correct the same, and henceforth strictly observe the proper recording of funds received to ensure reliability of reported balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the concerned offices to ensure that all funds received from source agencies NGAs and LGUs are supported with duly signed Memorandum of Agreement and/or related documents and a copy is furnished to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate and seek advice with the source agencies for the previously recorded Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs which remained outstanding for more than 10 years, in order to ascertain the proper treatment/usage of the funds and utilize the same as appropriate.

Xxx

Prepare the monthly Statement and Aging of Due to NGAs to ensure the proper monitoring of transferred funds and submit said reports to the Audit Team.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Agriculturist and concerned offices to submit the lacking documents mentioned, coordinate with the DA as to the proper implementation of the project, and henceforth ensure that there is proper utilization of funds in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement and pertinent documents.

We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Index Card from the Assessor’s Office and submit the same to Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth recognize in the books the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes as submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge - Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to:

Intensify its collection efforts for the uncollected stall fees and to henceforth ensure that all rentals are supported by a valid and binding lease contract;

Reconcile the variance noted in the monthly rates per master list of stall occupants and the Revised Revenue Code, and indicate the same in the Contract of Lease; and

Ensure that the granting of Contract of Lease as well as, the levy and collection of taxes and fees is in accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

Xxx

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and personnel responsible furnish immediately the COA Office copies of the Municipal Ordinance pertaining to stall rentals and Lease of Contracts to facilitate timely and relevant audit results.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan revisit the Municipal Ordinance to check whether rates and terms and conditions are still applicable and fair enough and update when found necessary to expand the implementation of revenue collection.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and the end-user units to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through implementation of the identified priority development projects in compliance with Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, and to oversee and closely monitor the timely implementation of these projects.

We also recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Development Council and end user units to henceforth ensure that all projects to be implemented during the year should be procurement-and-implementation ready to make certain its timely implementation and completion.

We recommended and it was agreed that: 

The MDRRM Council review and update the LDRRMFIP annually and include under a separate caption, the list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years and incorporate the same in the AIP of the Municipality;

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the PPAs are included in the AIP prior to the processing of disbursements; and

The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Monthly and Annual Report on Receipts and Utilization of the DRRM Fund in the prescribed format as required in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the MDRRM Council and other concerned officials agreed to reassess all PPAs included in the LDRRMF current and continuing appropriations which remained unimplemented to date and determine the necessity to implement the same otherwise, reprogram to priority PPAs and ensure its full implementation. It was also agreed that the concerned offices make certain the propriety of all charges against the fund, in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 in order to ensure the proper utilization of funds and to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We maintained our recommendation that the Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories, prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books and to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Human Resource Management Office, Municipal Accountant, and concerned offices agreed to:

Maintain a manpower database to effectively account and monitor its personnel complement and ensure that all outsourced workers are covered with a binding contract and/or other appropriate documents;



Formulate policies/guidelines in engaging and managing outsourced workers and in the granting of wages and allowances; and

Ensure that all accomplishments reported are quantifiable to allow verification of the same with the stated duties and functions per Contracts, and make certain that validation is made as to the number of days worked per payroll and DTR, with the stated working days as agreed upon in the Contracts, and all claims are supported with documents that are complete and proper before payment is made.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to henceforth comply with the requirements of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 in the preparation and submission of the GAD AR including the required supporting documents to enable the timely evaluation of the propriety of charges against the fund and the status of implementation of GAD PPAs to assess whether GAD mainstreaming was achieved and identified priority gender issues were addressed.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust as appropriate, the erroneous recordings made and henceforth, record the advance, progress and final payments for infrastructure projects using the appropriate asset accounts, in accordance with Section 81, Chapter 3, Volume III and Section 50, Chapter 3, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs, and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the Assets in the books using the appropriate asset account as well as, the corresponding depreciation, and henceforth ensure the proper recording of the infrastructure assets whether in the process of construction or already completed and the related depreciation, in accordance with Section 50, Chapter 3, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs, COA Circular No. 2015-009, and IPSAS 17.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Ensure that funds received are supported with duly signed MOA or related documents;
Coordinate and seek advice with the source agencies for the previously recorded Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs which remained outstanding for more than 10 years, to ascertain the propriety and purpose of the funds and implement and/or refund the balances as appropriate, otherwise, consider requesting for its write-off pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and
Reconcile the erroneous entries affecting the Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs accounts, and effect the necessary adjustments to ensure the correct and reliable presentation of the year-end balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Index Card from the Assessor’s Office and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth recognize in the books the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes as submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the amount recognized as Receivables and Deferred Income at year-end to exclude fines and penalties.

We maintained our audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to perform verification of the reconciling items and cause the preparation of adjusting journal entries to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements, and to henceforth comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the preparation and submission of the BRS to the Office of the Auditor.

We maintained our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Ensure that only transactions with valid claims and covered with perfected contracts and documents evidencing receipt of goods and services are recognized as Accounts Payable in the books of accounts; and

Henceforth ensure the proper recognition of payables and correctly record the same in the books using the appropriate liability accounts.

We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to monitor and maintain a comprehensive report on the collection of real property taxes due for the current year, design and implement more effective collection strategies to maximize collection efficiency, and enforce remedies provided under Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent RPT/SET.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to prepare the necessary documents and return without delay to the National Treasury the unutilized balance of ₱1.849 million, and validate the discrepancies noted in the reported liquidation in comparison with the duly signed payrolls for the ₱7.532 million of funds utilized to ascertain the propriety of the usage of funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and offices in-charge of fund distribution to communicate with the source agency and secure a copy of the MOA/appropriate documents to determine the proper accounting of the unutilized fund balance, and submit copies of all official receipts indicated in the payrolls to substantiate the reported refunds made.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth make certain that all liquidation reports and documents for all fund transfers are properly reviewed as to accuracy and completeness to promptly effect corrections before recording of liquidation in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

The concerned Rural Health Unit personnel prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances including its supporting documents, provide valuation for the identified ending inventory balances of the COVID-19 vaccines and its ancillary supplies, and furnish the copy of report to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations
We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendations and Management has agreed that:
a.	The Inventory Committee establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its properties by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE in compliance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386;
b.	The Municipal Accountant and GSO In-Charge maintain and regularly update their respective reports namely, PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; and
c.	The Municipal Accountant and GSO In-Charge perform a reconciliation of the reported PPEs per physical count, accounting and property records in order to establish the correct balance of the PPE accounts.

Recommendations
We maintained our audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the  Municipal Accountant to perform verification of the reconciling items and cause the preparation of adjusting journal entries to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements, and to henceforth comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the preparation and submission of the BRS to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations
We maintained our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Ensure that only transactions with valid claims and covered with perfected contracts and documents evidencing receipt of goods and services are recognized as Accounts Payable in the books of accounts; and
Henceforth ensure the proper recognition of payables and correctly record the same in the books using the appropriate liability accounts.

Recommendation
We maintained our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the proper recording of the transfer to the Trust Fund books of the unexpended balance of CY 2022 QRF and DRRM-MOOE and henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the accounting of unutilized balances of the LDRRMF.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the nature of the erroneous accounts and make the necessary adjustments to correct the same, and henceforth strictly observe the proper recording of funds received to ensure reliability of reported balances in the financial statements.

The lack of a sound system of internal control in the administration of funds received from source agencies NGAs and LGUs resulted to: a) absence of Memoranda of Agreement (MOA) and related documents contrary to Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and Item 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and b) unaccounted balances of Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs amounting to ₱0.559 million and ₱389,844.33 respectively hence, rendered it difficult to ascertain the proper usage of funds including the timeliness of implementation of funded programs, projects and activities.
Pertinent provisions of Section 4 of PD 1445 provides that:
“Xxx
4(3) Trust Funds shall be available and may be spent only for the specific purpose for which the trust was created or the funds received.
Xxx
4(6) Claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation.”
Item 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 prescribes the following revised guidelines and documentary requirements on fund transfers:
“3.0 Fund Transfers
3.1 Transfer of Funds to Implementing Agency
Fund transfer should be properly taken up in the books of both agencies, used only for the purpose intended, and properly accounted and reported (COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994).
3.1.2. Implementation and Liquidation
Implementing Agency:
Necessary supporting documents depending on the nature of transactions
Copy of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) /Trust Agreement
Copy of Official Receipt (OR) upon receipt of funds transferred
Copy of the OR issued by the Source Agency evidencing refund of unexpended/unutilized balance of fund transfer”
Audit of disbursements for funds received and recorded under Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts revealed that fund transfers totaling ₱35.382 million do not have their file of MOA and/or other related documents on record. The absence thereof rendered it difficult to ascertain the timeliness of implementation and propriety of utilization of funds. Details are summarized as follows:
(See details in Appendix I)

Inquiry with the Municipal Accountant disclosed that copies of the MOA were not furnished to his office after funds were transferred, and those pertaining to funds received from prior years were not turned over by the previous administration.
For funds received as Local Government Support Fund – Financial Assistance to LGUs totaling ₱12.0 million during CY 2021, proof of compliance to posting requirements and preparation of quarterly fund utilization and status of project implementation were not included in the vouchers submitted. Of the total amount, ₱2.0 million was received for the construction of access road project at Barangay Bularan which was completed on June 17, 2022. The ₱10.0 million that was received for the construction of farm to market road at Barangay Bobon was completed on June 27, 2022. Funds were fully utilized and any remaining balance was returned to the Bureau of Treasury.
Also included in the schedule above were funds received totaling ₱0.559 million and ₱389,844.33 respectively, which were credited to the Due to NGA and LGU accounts and has remained outstanding for more than 10 years which could no longer be accounted for. Details are shown in Appendix I.
The failure to establish a sound system of internal control in the administration of funds received which resulted to the absence of MOA and other related documents including the unaccounted previous years’ balances of the Due to LGUs and NGAs accounts rendered it difficult to determine the proper usage of funds, and the timeliness of implementation of funded programs and projects.
The audit observation was communicated to Management through Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2023-05(2022) dated April 14, 2023.
Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the concerned offices to ensure that all funds received from source agencies NGAs and LGUs are supported with duly signed Memorandum of Agreement and/or related documents and a copy is furnished to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate and seek advice with the source agencies for the previously recorded Due to LGUs and NGAs which remained outstanding for more than 10 years, in order to ascertain the proper treatment/usage of the funds and utilize the same as appropriate.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Agriculturist and concerned offices to submit the lacking documents mentioned, coordinate with the DA as to the proper implementation of the project, and henceforth ensure that there is proper utilization of funds in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement and pertinent documents.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Index Card from the Assessor’s Office and submit the same to Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth recognize in the books the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes as submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to monitor and maintain a comprehensive report on the collection of real property taxes due for the current year, design and implement more effective collection strategies to maximize collection efficiency, and enforce remedies provided under Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent RPT/SET.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge - Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to:
Intensify its collection efforts for the uncollected stall fees and to henceforth ensure that all rentals are supported by a valid and binding lease contract;
Reconcile the variance noted in the monthly rates per master list of stall occupants and the Revised Revenue Code, and indicate the same in the Contract of Lease; and
Ensure that the granting of Contract of Lease as well as, the levy and collection of taxes and fees is in accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the OIC – Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to prepare the necessary documents and return without delay to the National Treasury the unutilized balance of ₱1.849 million, and validate the discrepancies noted in the reported liquidation in comparison with the duly signed payrolls for the ₱7.532 million of funds utilized to ascertain the propriety of the usage of funds.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and offices in-charge of fund distribution to communicate with the source agency and secure a copy of the MOA/appropriate documents to determine the proper accounting of the unutilized fund balance, and submit copies of all official receipts indicated in the payrolls to substantiate the reported refunds made.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth make certain that all liquidation reports and documents for all fund transfers are properly reviewed as to accuracy and completeness to promptly effect corrections before recording of liquidation in the books.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and the end-user units to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through implementation of the identified priority development projects in compliance with Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, and to oversee and closely monitor the timely implementation of these projects.
We also recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Development Council and end user units to henceforth ensure that all projects to be implemented during the year should be procurement-and-implementation ready to make certain its timely implementation and completion.

Recommendations
We recommended and it was agreed that:
The MDRRM Council review and update the LDRRMFIP annually and include under a separate caption, the list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years and incorporate the same in the AIP of the Municipality;
The Municipal Accountant ensure that the PPAs are included in the AIP prior to the processing of disbursements; and
The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Monthly and Annual Report on Receipts and Utilization of the DRRM Fund in the prescribed format as required in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No 2013-1 and COA Circular 
No. 2012-002.

Recommendation
We recommended and the MDRRM Council and other concerned officials agreed to reassess all PPAs included in the LDRRMF current and continuing appropriations which remained unimplemented to date and determine the necessity to implement the same otherwise, reprogram to priority PPAs and ensure its full implementation. It was also agreed that the concerned offices make certain the propriety of all charges against the fund, in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 in order to ensure the proper utilization of funds and to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

Recommendation
We maintained our recommendation that the Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories, prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books and to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed that:
The concerned Rural Health Unit personnel prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances including its supporting documents, provide valuation for the identified ending inventory balances of the COVID-19 vaccines and its ancillary supplies, and furnish the copy of report to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
The Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the ending balance using the appropriate inventory account based on the report submitted, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Human Resource Management Office, Municipal Accountant, and concerned offices agreed to:
Maintain a manpower database to effectively account and monitor its personnel complement and ensure that all outsourced workers are covered with a binding contract and/or other appropriate documents;
Formulate policies/guidelines in engaging and managing outsourced workers and in the granting of wages and allowances; and
Ensure that all accomplishments reported are quantifiable to allow verification of the same with the stated duties and functions per Contracts, and make certain that validation is made as to the number of days worked per payroll and DTR, with the stated working days as agreed upon in the Contracts, and all claims are supported with documents that are complete and proper before payment is made.

Recommendations
We recommended and the GFPS agreed to henceforth comply with the requirements of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 in the preparation and submission of the GAD AR including the required supporting documents to enable the timely evaluation of the propriety of charges against the fund and the status of implementation of GAD PPAs to assess whether GAD mainstreaming was achieved and identified priority gender issues were addressed.

We recommended that the LSB prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality disburse the SEF pursuant to the provisions of Sections 99 and 100 of RA No. 7160 and Section 1 of RA No. 5447.
We also recommended that the LSB focus the utilization of the SEF in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson, and all the other members of the LDRRM Council, observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF.  The fund be strictly utilized according to the guidelines in order to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal DRRMO to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, in prescribed formats, as required under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
We also recommended that properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted within sixty (60) days from receipt of the AAR to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were prepared but were not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the BS as an alternative procedure in preparing the BRS on time.
Further, we recommended that the LCE provide additional staff to the Municipal Accountant to assist him in the preparation of the financial reports and supporting schedules/ documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund the unused cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular Nos. 96-004, 97-002 and 2012-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The amount of unliquidated cash advances has decreased to P8,995,053.14 as of December 31, 2016. 

This audit observation is reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: Also, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of their salaries until they have complied with the requirement.

Further, we recommended that no additional cash advances shall be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated as provided under the above-cited provisions of law and regulations of the COA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: The balance has decreased to P2,413,258.10 as of December 31, 2016 of which P1,522,539.65 was the accountability of the current OIC-Municipal Treasurer, and was deposited on January 2017. The remaining undeposited collections belong to the deceased previous Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the designated GSO and/or employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010, by submitting copy of contracts/POs to the Auditor within five (5) working days from its execution, and notifying the latter of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance of such deliveries.
observations.managementAction: There are still Contracts/POs/JOs that were not submitted/ furnished to  the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB prepare the annual SEF/school board budget, supported by PPAs and the Municipality disburse funds in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 5447.
observations.managementAction: There were still expenses for the year charged to the SEF which are not in accordance with RA No. 5447.

observations.recommendation: Also, we recommended that the LSB focus the utilization of the SEF in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to adhere strictly to the provisions of EO No. 298 regarding claims on travel expenses.
Also, we recommended that all government-owned motor vehicles be kept in the garage provided by the office.

We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS that shall take the lead in mainstreaming GAD in LGU PPAs.  The GFPS adhere strictly to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: Some of the provisions of RA No. 9003 were already implemented in the Municipality except for the establishment of a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to correct the proper classification of accounts based on the account descriptions provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009.  Likewise, the subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of working papers shall be submitted to support the conversion and/or reclassification of accounts in order to ascertain the correctness of its presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of working papers were not submitted to the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that The LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2014 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/ connected to their departments and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAPSI for CY 2014 was submitted to the COA but was not properly filled-up or accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned LGU officials see to it that payments of incentives for government personnel be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing payments of such to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.
We also recommended that the PEI received in excess of the authorized amount for each government employee of the Municipality be refunded.
observations.managementAction: Effective October 2016, the LGU officials and employees have   started  refunding  disallowed PEI in the amount of  P500.00 per person liable. For those who have separated from the service in CY 2016, their  full disallowed amounts were settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001, by supporting all disbursements with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: As per audit, there are still several disbursements with lacking supporting documents,    contrary to the prescribed regulations
